item.title: Formatting Currency in JavaScript
item.author: bnadlerjr@gmail.com (Bob Nadler, Jr.)
item.description: The other day I needed to format some currency values in JavaScript. I remembered coming across Intl.NumberFormat a long time ago on another project, but browser support was sketchy. I looked up the most recent info on caniuse and thankfully almost...

item.title: ClojureScript with Middleman via Shadow-CLJS
item.author: bnadlerjr@gmail.com (Bob Nadler, Jr.)
item.description: Ruby's Middleman is my preferred tool when I need to generate a static website. In this post I'm going to show you how to set up a Middleman project that uses ClojureScript instead of the default JavaScript or CoffeeScript that ships with Middleman...

item.title: boot-selmer
item.author: bnadlerjr@gmail.com (Bob Nadler, Jr.)
item.description:
Introduction

I’ve been playing around with Clojure and ClojureScript for awhile. So far I’ve always used Leiningen for managing dependencies and build tasks. I recently decided to give Boot a try. I needed to create a Boot task for compiling Selmer

item.title: xclip for Ubuntu
item.author: bnadlerjr@gmail.com (Bob Nadler, Jr.)
item.description: Awhile back I wrote about pbcopy for OS X. These days I find myself using Ubuntu quite often and missed having pbcopy. Turns out there is a package called xclip that does the same thing on Ubuntu! Give it a try!

item.title: Rails Data Migration Results
item.author: bnadlerjr@gmail.com (Bob Nadler, Jr.)
item.description:
When writing data migrations, it can be useful to know the number of records that were updated / created. If you run your migration in a Rails console, ActiveRecord will print out the database response object like this for PostgreSQL:

2.1.5 :008 >...

item.title: OS X pbcopy
item.author: bnadlerjr@gmail.com (Bob Nadler, Jr.)
item.description:
OS X has a nifty utility program named pbcopy that I just learned about which can be used to copy things to the system clipboard.

For example: cat foo.txt | pbcopy or pbcopy < foo.txt will copy the contents of foo.txt onto the system clipboard.

...
